questions.stem: This abdominal CT shows a well-circumscribed mass arising from the stomach wall and displacing the adjacent stomach, vessels, pancreas, and liver. This tumor most commonly arises from which cell type and driver mutation?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Enterochromaffin-like cells with a MEN1 mutation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Smooth muscle cells with a TP53 mutation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Interstitial cells of Cajal with a KIT gain-of-function mutation

choices.label: E
choices.text: B lymphocytes with H. pylori-driven proliferation

explanation.summary: A submucosal, well-circumscribed gastric mass is typical of a gastrointestinal stromal tumor (GIST), which arises from the interstitial cells of Cajal, most often via activating KIT (or PDGFRA) mutations.
explanation.whyCorrect: A submucosal, well-circumscribed gastric mass is typical of a gastrointestinal stromal tumor (GIST), which arises from the interstitial cells of Cajal, most often via activating KIT (or PDGFRA) mutations.

questions.stem: This endoscopic view of the stomach shows several small, smooth, dome-shaped mucosal nodules. These lesions are most commonly the result of which underlying process?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Chronic inflammation, often from H. pylori-associated gastritis

explanation.summary: These are hyperplastic gastric polyps, the most common gastric polyp. They arise from overactive/uncontrolled foveolar proliferation driven by chronic inflammation, classically chronic H. pylori gastritis, and are usually benign.
explanation.whyCorrect: These are hyperplastic gastric polyps, the most common gastric polyp. They arise from overactive/uncontrolled foveolar proliferation driven by chronic inflammation, classically chronic H. pylori gastritis, and are usually benign.

questions.stem: This endoscopic image of the gastric antrum shows a solitary, flat mucosal lesion. Compared with the far more common hyperplastic polyp, this type of lesion is distinguished by which feature?

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is dysplastic and premalignant, capable of progressing to adenocarcinoma

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is composed of mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue

choices.label: D
choices.text: It has essentially no malignant potential

choices.label: E
choices.text: It arises from interstitial cells of Cajal

explanation.summary: This is a gastric adenoma. Unlike hyperplastic polyps, adenomas are dysplastic by definition and are a recognized precursor to intestinal-type gastric adenocarcinoma; larger, flatter lesions carry the greatest risk.
explanation.whyCorrect: This is a gastric adenoma. Unlike hyperplastic polyps, adenomas are dysplastic by definition and are a recognized precursor to intestinal-type gastric adenocarcinoma; larger, flatter lesions carry the greatest risk.
